data.title: US Core PCE Inflation Set to Hit 5-Month High
data.description:
The US PCE price index is expected to rise 0.2% month-over-month in July 2025, down from June’s 0.3% gain.

Core PCE, which excludes food and energy, is projected to increase 0.3%, matching the prior month.

Annually, headline PCE inflation is seen steady at 2.6%, while core PCE is set to edge up to 2.9% from 2.8%, its highest in five months.

The PCE index is the Fed’s preferred inflation gauge.

data.title: Alibaba falls short of first-quarter revenue estimates
data.description:
China's Alibaba , missed market estimates for quarterly revenue on Friday as the company's e-commerce business grapples with tough competition and choppy consumer demand, eclipsing gains in its cloud computing business.

U.S.-listed shares of the company fell 1.5% in premarket trading.

Consumer confidence in China has taken a beating from persistent weakness, with the economy weighed down by a lingering property sector crisis, weak wage growth and global trade disruptions.

Consumers remain cautious, even as e-commerce companies resorted to steep discounts and price cuts to drum up demand.

That eclipsed strong growth in Alibaba's cloud segment, where revenue surged 26% to 33.40 billion yuan, accelerating from the 18% growth seen in the prior quarter. Analysts were expecting an 18.4% rise to 31.44 billion yuan, according to LSEG data.

Alibaba has been among the most aggressive players in China's AI landscape, unveiling upgrades on an almost weekly basis, most recently rolling out a model that converts portrait photos into lifelike video avatars.

The company reported total revenue of 247.65 billion yuan ($34.62 billion) in the first quarter ended June, compared with an estimate of 252.92 billion yuan, according to data compiled by LSEG.

($1 = 7.1529 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: REG - Dewhurst Group PLC - Result of Tender Offer and Total Voting Rights
data.description:
RNS Number : 2438X Dewhurst Group PLC 29 August 2025  

This release contains inside information within the meaning of Article 7(1) of the EU Market Abuse Regulation (2014/596/EU), or EU MAR, and of the UK version of EU MAR as it forms part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act (as amended).

29 August 2025

Dewhurst Group plc

("Dewhurst", the "Group" or "the Company")

Result of Tender Offer and Total Voting Rights

Dewhurst Group plc, the global manufacturer and supplier of quality components to the lift, transport and keypad industries, announces the results of the Tender Offer set out in the circular published by the Company on 5 August (the "Circular"), which closed at 1.00 p.m. on 28 August 2025. The Tender Price was £6.65 per 'A' Share and £9.00 per Ordinary Share.

The Company offered to purchase up to a maximum of 3,739,897 Shares. As set out in the Circular, under the Basic Entitlement, the Company offered to repurchase 3,007,518 'A' Shares and 555,555 Ordinary Shares. In total, 1,869,456 'A' Shares and 517,802 Ordinary Shares were validly tendered under the Tender Offer through Qualifying Shareholders' Basic Entitlements as well as excess applications received in respect of 275,326 'A' Shares and 452,905 Ordinary Shares. All valid tenders were satisfied in full pursuant to their Basic Entitlement (rounded down to the nearest whole number of Shares) and excess applications were also satisfied in full, with 275,326 'A' Shares and 452,905 Ordinary Share accepted pursuant to excess tenders.

It is expected that cheques will be despatched and CREST accounts will be credited with proceeds in respect of successfully tendered shares by 10 September 2025.

Of the 2,144,782 'A' Shares and 970,707 Ordinary Shares validly tendered under the Tender Offer 2,144,782 'A' Shares and 920,707 Ordinary Shares will be repurchased by the Company under the Repurchase Agreement and cancelled, with the balance of 50,000 Ordinary Shares to be repurchased by the Company under the Repurchase Agreement and held in treasury.

The issued share capital of the Company following the purchase will be 2,280,736 'A' Shares and 2,388,493 Ordinary Shares (with 50,000 Ordinary Shares held in treasury). The total voting rights in the Company following the purchase and cancellation will be 2,338,493.

The figures of 2,280,736 'A' Shares and 2,338,493 Ordinary Shares may be used by Shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

The Company reminds Shareholders of the expected timetable of principal events as follows:

CREST accounts settled in respect of unsold tendered Shares held in uncertificated form

2 September

Payments through CREST made in respect of Shares held in uncertificated form successfully tendered

by 10 September

Cheques despatched in respect of Shares held in certificated form successfully tendered

by 10 September

Balancing certificates despatched in respect of unsold tendered

by 10 September

Last day of dealings in the Ordinary Shares and 'A' Shares on AIM

10 September

Cancellation of admission of the Ordinary Shares and 'A' Shares to trading on AIM

 11 September

Re-registration as a private company

Expected by 26 September

Unless otherwise defined, capitalised terms in this announcement shall have the meaning set out in the Circular.

Contacts for further enquiries:

Dewhurst Group Plc

Tel: +44 (0)208 744 8200

Richard Dewhurst, Chairman

www.dewhurst-group.com

Jeremy Dewhurst, Chief Financial Officer

Cavendish Capital Markets Limited (Financial Adviser)

data.title: Kepler upgrades Adesso to "buy", shares rise
data.description:
** Shares in Adesso are up 4%, with analysts pointing to Kepler upgrading the company to "buy" from "hold"

** The broker expects the German IT services & software group to benefit from seven additional working days in H2, improving utilisation and license contributions

** Kepler adds budget releases, which were delayed after the German election, should start to materialize after H2

** From H2 onwards the broker also expects "gradual profitability from the Indian shoring ramp-up"

** Adesso is the top gainer of the German small-cap index ,

data.title: Israeli military says local tactical pause will not apply to Gaza City
data.description:
The Israeli military said that starting from Friday, a local humanitarian pause in military activity will not apply to the area of Gaza City, as it "constitutes a dangerous combat zone".

Israel's security cabinet had approved a plan to take control of Gaza City, a move expanding military operations in the shattered Palestinian territory that drew strong criticism at home and abroad over its pursuit of the almost two-year-old war.

Last month, Israel announced a halt in military operations for 10 hours a day in parts of Gaza and new aid corridors as Jordan and the United Arab Emirates airdropped supplies into the enclave.

The Israeli military said on Friday that it "will continue to support humanitarian efforts alongside ongoing maneuvering and offensive operations against terrorist organizations in the Gaza Strip in order to protect the state of Israel".

Israel has been facing growing international criticism, which the government rejects, over the humanitarian crisis in Gaza, and indirect ceasefire talks in Doha between Israel and the Palestinian militant group Hamas have broken off with no deal in sight.

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has said there will be no end to the war until Hamas is disarmed.

The war began on October 7, 2023, when Hamas-led fighters stormed southern Israel, killing 1,200 people, mostly civilians, and taking 251 hostages back to Gaza, according to Israeli tallies.

Since then, Israel's offensive has killed over 62,000 people in Gaza, mostly civilians, according to Gaza health officials, reduced much of the enclave to ruins and displaced nearly the entire population.

data.title: U.S. PCE Inflation Seen as Driver for Treasurys, Dollar — Market Talk
data.description:
0950 GMT - U.S. Treasury yields mostly edge higher ahead of closely-watched PCE inflation data for July at 1230 GMT. The data, which is the Federal Reserve's preferred gauge of inflation, will also be a driver for the dollar. "Financial markets will be guided today by the U.S. July PCE data," Brown Brothers Harriman's Elias Haddad says in a note. "The rising ISM prices index and weakening in the labor market point to upside risk to PCE inflation and downside risk to real personal spending," the senior markets strategist says. The 10-year Treasury yield rises 2.1 basis points to 4.227%, while the 30-year yield is up 3.6bps at 4.907%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0922 GMT - Eurozone sovereigns have completed 74% of their annual government bond issuance needs by the end of August, LBBW's Elmar Voelker says in a note. Year-to-date gross government bond issuance by the eurozone's 11 largest issuers stands at 985 billion euros, about 2% ahead of last year's completion progress at the same point in time, the senior fixed income analyst says. LBBW forecasts this year's gross government bond issuance by the 11 largest issuers at 1.33 trillion euros, compared to last year's 1.30 trillion euros, he says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0915 GMT - A looming supply surplus hangs over the oil market, but its extent largely depends on the evolution of the demand outlook, says Commerzbank Research's Barbara Lambrecht. If sentiment indicators for China, Europe, and the U.S. improve more than most analysts expect in the coming days, the global consumption outlook could also brighten. "After all, most countries are benefiting not only from the decline in oil prices, but also from the depreciation of the U.S. dollar," the commodity analyst says. In euros, a barrel of Brent crude is currently about 20% cheaper than at the start of the year, according to the firm. Plus, strong travel activity over the upcoming Labor Day weekend in the U.S. could further boost demand. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0908 GMT - Upward pressure on gilt yields should ease over the coming year, Berenberg's Andrew Wishart says in a note. The government's decision to raise employers' payroll tax should lower the U.K. debt to GDP ratio and allay fiscal concerns, he says. In addition, U.K. inflation is expected to decelerate in 2026, enabling the Bank of England to cut interest rates further, he says. These developments should stop gilt yields from rising further, Wishart says. The 10-year gilt yield rises 2 basis point to last trade at 4.721%, Tradeweb data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0908 GMT - The Reserve Bank of Australia is likely to delay its rate cutting path after an expectedly high July CPI reading, says Julius Baer's Fixed Income Analyst Asia Magdalene Teo. The private banking firm views the spike, driven by an electricity rebate extension in New South Wales and the Capital Territory ending in August and peak summer holiday spending, as likely to be transitory. It expects inflation will retreat toward the lower end of the central bank's 2%-3% target band this month. Still, the index's volatility supports the central bank's approach to slow down on rate cuts to avoid inflation, and Teo notes that market has priced in little chance of a September rate cut and two more cuts by year end.(jason.chau@wsj.com)

0903 GMT - USD/INR could hit 89 amid persistent of U.S. trade tensions, the Mizuho Macro Research team writes in a note. Tariff risks have driven the rupee to tumble since end-July despite softer oil prices and positive GST reforms, they write. While tensions could ease, India is keeping strategic flexibility on Russian oil and prioritizing protection of its farm sector in U.S. trade talks rather than yielding to all U.S. demands, they say. Bottoming inflation and deeper rate cuts by the central bank could also weigh on the rupee's attractiveness, it says, adding the USD/INR may see some alleviation towards the 85 level in 2026. The Indian rupee hit an all-time low Friday, with USD/INR at 88.2960, LSEG data show. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0849 GMT - The Federal Reserve could prioritize growth over inflation in the coming years and such a scenario could support risk assets in the near term, BlueBay CIO Mark Dowding says in a note. "We would not be too surprised if the next few years see a FOMC that is more inclined to prioritise growth over inflation," he says. However, there could be more of a question mark over valuations if policy easing over the next 12 months results in the Fed later needing to tighten rates more aggressively to control price risks, Dowding says. The two-year Treasury yield is flat at 3.635%, while the 10-year yield is up 2 basis piont at 4.225%, accoridng to LSEG. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0847 GMT - Things will get worse before they get better for the creaking German jobs market, ING's Carsten Brzeski warns in a note to clients. The number of jobseekers topped 3 million as the unemployment rate climbed, figures from Germany's statistics office showed Friday. That "provide[s] further evidence that a long period of economic stagnation eventually takes a toll on the labour market," Brzeski says. With major industries setting out cost-cutting measures and layoffs, the country's jobs market has little hope of a quick rebound, he says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0840 GMT - The Malaysian ringgit may test 4.20 against the U.S. dollar next week, Kenanga economists say in a note. Softer nonfarm payrolls are expected with unemployment edge up to 4.3%, could weigh on the greenback, though the coming core PCE must not surprise on the upside, they say. U.S. President Trump's repeated attacks on the Fed risk undermining the central bank's independence, keeping dollar sentiment fragile until Powell's departure next year, they add. With Bank Negara likely to hold rates next week, Kenanga sees room for further gains in the ringgit. Kenanga expects USD/MYR to face resistance at 4.23 and support at 4.20 in the short term. USD/MYR is 0.2% higher at 4.2230. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0827 GMT - Euro-denominated credit is likely to experience increased volatility in the near term due to political uncertainty in France, Barclays credit strategists say in a note. Companies are also likely to increase issuance in September, raising supply in the euro credit market, the strategists say. "We expect further acceleration [in issuance] in September but think volumes will be manageable," they say. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0810 GMT - i moved the forward looking bits to the top India's broader economic growth outlook remains sluggish as U.S. tariffs harm labor intensive sectors like textiles, precipitating job losses and factory shutdowns, say Nomura research analysts Aurodeep Nandi and Sonal Varma. Even though India's July industrial production growth at 3.5% beat market expectations of 2.3%, they think this may reflect front-loaded exports to the U.S. before the U.S.'s initial 25% tariffs on all Indian goods introduced this month, which is now raised to 50%. Nomura thinks U.S. tariffs will harm labor intensive sectors like textiles, precipitating job losses and factory shutdowns. Nomura expects 2Q GDP growth to weaken to 6.9% on year from 1Q's 7.4%, and trims its full year growth forecast to 6.0% from 6.2%. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

0806 GMT - Gold futures inch lower but are on track to end the week significantly higher on mounting expectations of a September U.S. interest-rate cut. Futures are down 0.1% at $3,470.20 a troy ounce but are up 1.5% on week. The precious metal has continued to gain after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech at the Jackson Hole symposium last week, in which he cautiously opened the door to easing monetary policy, XS.com's Linh Tran says in a note. The latest U.S. economic data has made the picture more complex and could temper gold's rally, though geopolitical instability continues to support safe-haven demand for bullion, Tran says. The medium-term outlook for gold is cautiously positive, though short-term gold prices may fluctuate sharply on economic data, Tran says. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

data.title: China's major lenders report weaker profit margins as rate cuts weigh
data.description:
Key points:
  • Shrinking margins show signs of stress for China's big banks
  • ICBC net interest margin slips to 1.3%
  • Non-performing loan ratio holds steady
  • Uneven economy facing fresh pressures from US tariffs

China's major state-owned banks reported weaker first-half net interest margins amid lower interest rates as the world's second largest economy grapples with deflation and global economic volatility.

The Chinese banking sector's profit margins have been under pressure since the COVID-19 pandemic, weighed down by successive central bank interest rate cuts to boost the slowing economy.

Industrial and Commercial Bank of China's (ICBC) , China Construction Bank Corp , , China's Bank of Communications Co Ltd , Bank of China Ltd , and Aricultural Bank of China's all reported a net interest rate margin decline in the past six months.

Chinese banks' net interest margin (NIM) - a key gauge of profitability - shrunk to a record low of 1.42% as of end-June, official data showed, below a 1.8% threshold regarded in the industry as necessary to maintain reasonable profitability.

The five banks posted their first-half earnings on Friday after Hong Kong financial markets closed.

Net profit results among the group were mixed, with Agricultural Bank recording a 2.7% lift and Bank of Communications reporting a 1.61% increase which was well above analysts' forecasts.

The remaining three said net profit fell between 0.85% and 1.4%. China Construction Bank's decline came against analysts' expectations that its net profit would increase.

The reports also showed non-performing loan values remained relatively flat in the past six months, despite concerns about deteriorating asset quality, especially for struggling smaller businesses and consumers.

In a move to bolster the banking sector's stability and ensure flow of credit to the economically-crucial sectors, four of China's largest state lenders in March unveiled around a $72 billion recapitalisation plan.

($1 = 7.1529 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Thailand's Paetongtarn says she tried to safeguard country's interests
data.description:
Thailand's former Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra said on Friday she had sought to safeguard the country's interests in a phone conversation with Cambodia's former leader that led to her dismissal by the Constitutional Court.

"The verdict today caused a change in Thai politics, we all have to help, all sides, whether government or opposition, or the people, all of us have to work together to build political stability and to ensure that there won't be another turning point again," she told reporters.

data.description:
RNS Number : 2436X Sabre Insurance Group PLC 29 August 2025  

29 August 2025

Sabre Insurance Group plc

Total Voting Rights

For the purposes of DTR5.6.1R of the Transparency Directive, the Company notifies the market of the following:

Sabre Insurance Group ("Sabre" or the "Company") today announces that as at 29 August 2025, the Company's issued and fully paid share capital consists of 247,680,999 ordinary shares with a nominal value of £0.001 each ("Ordinary Shares"), with one voting right per share. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in Treasury.

 The total number of voting rights in the Company is therefore 247,680,999.

The above figure of 247,680,999 should be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company, under the Disclosure and Transparency Rules.

Anneka Kingan - Company Secretary

data.title: UK Market Talk Roundup: Shares Gaining
data.description:
Stocks on the rise in the UK today. Compiled by Dow Jones Newswires Markets Desk, markets.eu@dowjones.com

0939 GMT - BP doesn't intend to deploy large amounts of capital expenditure on more upstream projects, RBC Capital Markets analysts write after speaking to finance chief Kate Thomson. This is despite BP's considerable exploration success this year, of which Bumerangue in Brazil is the most significant, they say. The decision to not deploy significant capital on its growing set of exploration options seems to be part of the latest strategic review being conducted by the energy major's incoming chair, the analysts write. Its discovery in Brazil, as well as some of its Gulf of Mexico assets, are fully owned by BP and offer the opportunity to sell interests in the discoveries, they add. Shares trade up 0.9% at 432.95 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0906 GMT - BP's strategic review of Castrol continues to progress at speed and the lubricants business remains a prime asset for disposal, RBC Capital Markets analysts write after speaking to finance chief Kate Thomson. The sale of Castrol and solar business Lightsource BP would help the company meet its net-debt targets, they write. BP continues to expect $3 billion to $4 billion in divestment proceeds this year, with $3 billion in sales already agreed, they add. The oil major has so far received $1.7 billion in proceeds and expects most of the remaining amount in the fourth quarter of the year, they write. BP has other sales that it expects to complete by the end of the year, they write. Shares trade up 1.2% at 434.30 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)
